adds.rationale: Diversified thematic ETF for the AI + robotics wave, complementing the mega-cap AI incumbents already held (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL) with mid-cap industrial-automation and autonomy names.

news_evidence.summary: BOTZ tracks the Indxx Global Robotics & AI Thematic Index; coverage in early-to-mid 2021 highlighted its ~46% trailing 12-month return and growing investor interest as a clean way to bet on automation and AI adoption.

Global X positions BOTZ as a thematic vehicle for companies that stand to benefit from increased adoption of robotics and AI, including industrial robotics, non-industrial robots, and autonomous vehicles.

adds.rationale: ARK Space Exploration & Innovation ETF, launched 2021-03-30, gives early exposure to the rockets/spacecraft next-wave (orbital and suborbital platforms, enabling tech, beneficiaries) while individual pure-play space stocks are still scarce on US exchanges.

news_evidence.summary: ARK Invest launched ARKX on 2021-03-30, its first new ETF in two years, targeting orbital aerospace, suborbital aerospace, enabling technologies (3D printing, robotics, AI), and aerospace beneficiaries.
Virgin Galactic confirmed Richard Branson would fly on Unity 22 on 2021-07-11, accelerating the public narrative around commercial human spaceflight in the days before the as-of date.

adds.rationale: VanEck Semiconductor ETF concentrates the AI-hardware picks-and-shovels trade (NVDA, TSM, ASML, AMD) that directly captures hyperscaler datacenter capex; complements broad-market SPY with high-conviction AI silicon beta during the ongoing chip shortage.

news_evidence.summary: Global semiconductor shortage through H1 2021 underscored demand for advanced chips across autos, datacenter, and consumer electronics, with TSMC accelerating capacity build-out and AI/datacenter demand cited as a structural tailwind.
SMH (VanEck Semiconductor ETF) is widely used as the cleanest US-listed proxy for semiconductor exposure, with top holdings TSM, NVDA, ASML, and AMD that are direct AI-compute beneficiaries.

rationale_overall: Net effect: the watchlist gets its first deliberate thematic spine. AAPL/MSFT/GOOGL remain as mega-cap AI incumbents, SPY stays as broad-market beta, AGG as the bond ballast. Three thematic ETFs are added: BOTZ broadens AI/robotics beyond US mega-caps, SMH concentrates the AI-silicon picks-and-shovels trade, and ARKX is an early, cheap toehold in the rockets/spacecraft next-wave while pure-play space tickers are still scarce on US exchanges. Watchlist size grows from 5 to 8, well under the cap of 12.
